creator: Liviu.Ionescu@Romania.EU.net
text:
> The third problem is that the user appears to have a cabling, parity or
> termination problem on his SCSI chain.  That can't be fixed in software.

right. the cd-rom was hooked on the 8 bit cable of scsi channel B. although
it was alone and properly terminated, the other end of the bus was not
terminated (neither the 16 bit cable had a terminated device nor the
motherboard terminator was enabled).

enabling the motherboard termination fixed the problem.

NB: the Intel DK440 BIOS setup stated that enabling the internal termination
for channel B will allow only 16bit devices to be hooked, which doesn't seem
true (this is the reason I missed it in the beginning).


regards,

Liviu Ionescu
